title: Use errors.New() instead of Wrapf() for new errors
body: Call `errors.New()` instead of calling `errors.Wrapf()` when there's no error to wrap.

comments.body:
Why not go all the way
```
 grep -r fmt.Errorf . | grep -v vendor | grep -v .git
./user_unix_cgo.go:		return nil, fmt.Errorf("%q is not a regular file", ctrfile)
./image.go:		return nil, fmt.Errorf("no supported manifest types (attempted to use %q, only know %q and %q)",
./image.go:				return nil, fmt.Errorf("error storing layer %q to file: inconsistent layer size (copied %d, wrote %d)", layerID, size, counter.Count)
./image.go:	return nil, fmt.Errorf("can't write to a container")
./image.go:	return []byte{}, "", fmt.Errorf("TODO")

./cmd/buildah/common.go:		return nil, fmt.Errorf("error mocking up build configuration")

./cmd/buildah/from.go:		return fmt.Errorf("too many arguments specified")
./cmd/buildah/addcopy.go:		return fmt.Errorf("container ID must be specified")
./cmd/buildah/tag.go:		return fmt.Errorf("image name and at least one new name must be specified")
./cmd/buildah/rmi.go:		return fmt.Errorf("image name or ID must be specified")
./cmd/buildah/config.go:		return fmt.Errorf("container ID must be specified")
./cmd/buildah/config.go:		return fmt.Errorf("too many arguments specified")
Binary file ./buildah matches
./buildah.go:		return nil, fmt.Errorf("container is not a %s container", Package)
./add.go:		return fmt.Errorf("error reading contents for %q: wrong length (%d != %d)", destination, n, resp.ContentLength)
./add.go:		return fmt.Errorf("%q already exists, but is not a subdirectory)", filepath.Dir(dest))
./add.go:		return fmt.Errorf("destination %q is not a directory", dest)
./config.go:		return docker.V2Image{}, fmt.Errorf("error parsing image configuration from manifest")
```

Well, my goal was to fix compile errors, but yeah, why not.  Updated.
